narration: A normal letter follows one next child, but a dot means any one letter can fit there.
Because a dot can match many children, the search may branch into several possible paths.
decision: This is why recursion helps. It can try one child path, then another.

predictionPrompt.prompt: When the pattern starts with '.', what should the search do?

choices.label: Try all children
choices.feedback: Correct. The dot can match any one next letter.

choices.label: Stop right away
choices.feedback: Not this one. A dot means more choices, not failure.

narration: The search succeeds if any one of the possible child paths reaches a full word ending.
callout: Only one matching path has to work.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why can a wildcard turn one straight search into a branching search?
